explanation: This command triggers the Sigma rule by using the 'start' command with the '-launch' parameter to execute an arbitrary binary (notepad.exe).
This command uses the long-form option '--launch' instead of '-launch' to bypass the rule's detection based on short-form flags.
This command uses 'Sysnative' as an alternative path to System32, which may bypass detection by altering the binary path.
This command uses path normalization with multiple dots and newline characters to obfuscate the binary path and evade detection.
This command uses an environment variable to represent the path, which may bypass the rule by avoiding direct string matching.
This command adds an additional flag to the command line, which may help evade detection by increasing complexity and altering the string pattern.
